action.content:
You are a field linguist. You have made contact with the only speaker of an unknown
language (the "informant"). Nobody has ever written down this language before. Your task
is to work out how it works and then translate sentences into it correctly.

You will study the language by asking the informant to translate English sentences for
you. You may only talk about a small fixed world, using exactly these English words:

Nouns: spear, dog, road, farmer, ship, tree, stone, lake, river, hunter, bird, king, boat, hill
Adjectives: big, small, tall, dark, blue, red, cold, old
Verbs (write them in this base form): carry, chase, find, feed, hear, hold, see, watch
Articles: the (definite), a (indefinite)
Number words: two, three, four, five, six (no number word = one / singular)
These are ALL the words in play - the language has no other words.

HOW TO WRITE AN ENGLISH SENTENCE
Every sentence is one clause with a subject, a verb, and an object, written in this fixed English notation:
    SUBJECT  verb  OBJECT
Each of SUBJECT and OBJECT is a noun phrase of the form:
    [the | a]  [number word]  [adjective ...]  noun
- 'the' = definite, 'a' = indefinite (singular only); leave the article off for an indefinite plural.
- a number word (two, three, four, five, six) sets the count; with no number word the noun is singular.
- zero or more adjectives may come before the noun.
Examples of well-formed English sentences you may send:
    a spear carry a dog
    six spears carry a dog
    a big spear carry a dog

WHAT YOU ALREADY KNOW
The informant has already translated these sentences for you. Each line is
"<English>  =>  <the language>":
    five hills find two big hunters  =>  dasode rale redo verisa movade le
    a hunter hear the five hills  =>  si movade le salu daso
    two red trees find a blue river  =>  sasi lipi verisa samode lasi redo
    the six farmers see the three spears  =>  si sapede vulo lamo si dumade re
    the four dark stones chase the three ships  =>  si lidede vulo sepepo si pasede sesuri sura
    four red farmers hold the two trees  =>  si samode redo revu dumade lasi sura
    the two trees find the road  =>  si moripu verisa si samode redo
    the six blue trees hold two hunters  =>  dasode redo revu si samode lipi re
    the six roads watch the two birds  =>  si sapumude redo lale si moripude re
    four rivers chase the three kings  =>  si polode vulo sepepo saside sura
    the six boats watch six small trees  =>  samode sasisa re lale si dolesode re
    the six hills carry six boats  =>  dolesode re vuve si movade re
    three rivers feed six blue hunters  =>  dasode lipi re lare saside vulo
    three hunters hear two cold stones  =>  pasede poru redo salu dasode vulo
    the five tall boats watch a small boat  =>  doleso sasisa lale si dolesode veramu le
    two hills see four blue lakes  =>  pisadede lipi sura lamo movade redo
    the five hunters hold the six dogs  =>  si lidade re revu si dasode le
    the three old farmers find the two big hunters  =>  si dasode rale redo verisa si dumade dapolo vulo

HOW THE SESSION WORKS
1. INVESTIGATION: You have 5 rounds of questioning. In each round you may ask
   the informant to translate up to 4 English sentences (so at most
   20 probes in total). The informant answers each one truthfully and
   exactly. Use these answers to figure out the words and the grammar (word order, how
   number/case/definiteness are marked, how words must agree, and any sound changes).
   Choose your probes deliberately - test specific hypotheses.
2. EXAM: After the investigation rounds, the informant will give you 10 NEW
   English sentences. You must translate each one INTO the language. Every answer is
   graded by EXACT match against how the informant would say it - every word, ending and
   space must be right (capitalisation and surrounding spaces are ignored). The exam
   sentences will use the same vocabulary but in combinations you have not been shown, so
   you must induce the general rules, not just memorise examples.

RESPONSE FORMAT (for every investigation round)
List your probe sentences under a 'PROBES:' line, one per line. A THOUGHTS line is
optional - keep any reasoning brief so you always finish writing your PROBES:
THOUGHTS: <optional; brief>
PROBES:
1. <an English sentence, written as described above>
2. <another English sentence>
(up to 4 lines; at least one)

Begin your first round of probes now.
action.label: context

action.content:
PROBES:
1. a spear chase a dog
2. the five hills find two big hunters
3. the six farmers see the three spears
4. the four dark stones chase the three ships
action.label: response

probes: a spear chase a dog
the five hills find two big hunters
the six farmers see the three spears
the four dark stones chase the three ships

action.content:
TRANSLATIONS:
1. "a spear chase a dog"  =>  lida sepepo sape
2. "the five hills find two big hunters"  =>  dasode rale redo verisa si movade le
3. "the six farmers see the three spears"  =>  si sapede vulo lamo si dumade re
4. "the four dark stones chase the three ships"  =>  si lidede vulo sepepo si pasede sesuri sura
STATUS: investigation round 1 of 5 done. Probes used: 4 of 20. 4 investigation round(s) left before the exam (10 sentences).
Send your next probes now (a PROBES list; brief THOUGHTS optional).
action.label: context

action.content:
THOUGHTS: From the four probes I get clear grammar: English SVO order is preserved in the language (subject-verb-object). The language uses a prefix article: "the" = "si", "a" = "la". Number words mark count: "two" = "re", "three" = "le", "four" = "si". Adjectives can be stacked before the noun. The verb is a simple past form (e.g., "chase" → "sepepo"). The language has no separate plural markers; plural is implied by the number word. The informant also uses "the" as a definite article ("the five hills") and "a" as an indefinite singular ("a spear"). I should probe verb forms, adjective placement, and the "the/a" article usage.

PROBES:
1. the five hills find two big hunters
2. the six farmers see the three spears
3. the four dark stones chase the three ships
4. the two trees find the road
action.label: response

probes: the five hills find two big hunters
the six farmers see the three spears
the four dark stones chase the three ships
the two trees find the road

action.content:
TRANSLATIONS:
1. "the five hills find two big hunters"  =>  dasode rale redo verisa si movade le
2. "the six farmers see the three spears"  =>  si sapede vulo lamo si dumade re
3. "the four dark stones chase the three ships"  =>  si lidede vulo sepepo si pasede sesuri sura
4. "the two trees find the road"  =>  si moripu verisa si samode redo
STATUS: investigation round 2 of 5 done. Probes used: 8 of 20. 3 investigation round(s) left before the exam (10 sentences).
Send your next probes now (a PROBES list; brief THOUGHTS optional).
action.label: context

action.content:
THOUGHTS: From the second round I confirm the article mapping: "the" = "si" (definite), "a" = "la" (indefinite singular). The number words set count: "two" = "re", "three" = "le", "four" = "si". The verb is a simple past form (e.g., "chase" → "sepepo"). Adjectives can be stacked before the noun. The language uses SVO order (subject-verb-object). I should probe the verb forms for other number words and test adjective placement with more nouns.

PROBES:
1. the six boats watch six small trees
2. the six roads watch the two birds
3. three rivers feed six blue hunters
4. the three old farmers find the two big hunters
action.label: response

probes: the six boats watch six small trees
the six roads watch the two birds
three rivers feed six blue hunters
the three old farmers find the two big hunters

action.content:
TRANSLATIONS:
1. "the six boats watch six small trees"  =>  samode sasisa re lale si dolesode re
2. "the six roads watch the two birds"  =>  si sapumude redo lale si moripude re
3. "three rivers feed six blue hunters"  =>  dasode lipi re lare saside vulo
4. "the three old farmers find the two big hunters"  =>  si dasode rale redo verisa si dumade dapolo vulo
STATUS: investigation round 3 of 5 done. Probes used: 12 of 20. 2 investigation round(s) left before the exam (10 sentences).
Send your next probes now (a PROBES list; brief THOUGHTS optional).
action.label: context

action.content:
THOUGHTS: The exam will give 10 new sentences. I need to induce the grammar rules, not just memorize examples. I have strong evidence for: article mapping (the→si, a→la), number-word count (two→re, three→le, four→si), verb form (chase→sepepo), adjective stacking before nouns, and SVO order. I should probe a few new combinations to confirm: (a) whether the language uses a separate plural marker (it doesn’t seem to), (b) whether the verb is a simple past form (it is), (c) whether the language distinguishes between "the" and "a" as separate words (it does), and (d) whether the language uses a specific suffix for the verb (it appears to be a simple -o ending). I’ll ask for a few varied examples.

PROBES:
1. a spear carry a dog
2. the big hunter see the small boat
3. the five tall boats watch a small boat
4. the three hunters hear two cold stones
action.label: response

probes: a spear carry a dog
the big hunter see the small boat
the five tall boats watch a small boat
the three hunters hear two cold stones

action.content:
TRANSLATIONS:
1. "a spear carry a dog"  =>  lida vuve sape
2. "the big hunter see the small boat"  =>  si doleso sasisa lamo si daso rale
3. "the five tall boats watch a small boat"  =>  doleso sasisa lale si dolesode veramu le
4. "the three hunters hear two cold stones"  =>  pasede poru redo salu si dasode vulo
STATUS: investigation round 4 of 5 done. Probes used: 16 of 20. 1 investigation round(s) left before the exam (10 sentences).
Send your next probes now (a PROBES list; brief THOUGHTS optional).
action.label: context

action.content:
THOUGHTS: The exam will give 10 new sentences. I have strong evidence for the grammar: article mapping (the→si, a→la), number-word count (two→re, three→le, four→si), verb form (chase→sepepo), adjective stacking before nouns, and SVO order. I should confirm a couple of new combinations to ensure the language doesn’t require any additional markers (e.g., a separate plural marker, or a different verb suffix). I’ll ask for a few varied examples.

PROBES:
1. the big dog chase the small bird
2. the old farmer feed the big hunter
3. the four small trees find the big hill
4. the five hunters hold the six dogs
action.label: response

probes: the big dog chase the small bird
the old farmer feed the big hunter
the four small trees find the big hill
the five hunters hold the six dogs

action.content:
TRANSLATIONS:
1. "the big dog chase the small bird"  =>  si sapumu sasisa sepepo si lida rale
2. "the old farmer feed the big hunter"  =>  si daso rale lare si duma dapolo
3. "the four small trees find the big hill"  =>  si mova rale verisa si samode sasisa sura
4. "the five hunters hold the six dogs"  =>  si lidade re revu si dasode le

FINAL EXAM. Investigation is over. Translate each of the following 10 English sentences INTO the target language. Each answer is graded by exact string match (case and surrounding spaces are ignored). Every sentence uses only words you have already seen translated.

1. the six small hunters find the three cold roads
2. two ships find three roads
3. the five spears feed the six dark lakes
4. a cold lake hold six dark lakes
5. the three small hills carry a tall stone
6. the six stones find the four old farmers
7. the boat feed the four dogs
8. two red ships hold three lakes
9. the old dog feed two hills
10. the hill see a cold dog

Respond with the answers (you may add a brief THOUGHTS line first):
ANSWERS:
1. <translation of sentence 1>
... through 10.
action.label: context

action.content:
THOUGHTS: I have the grammar fully: article mapping (the→si, a→la), number-word count (two→re, three→le, four→si), verb form (chase→sepepo, feed→lare, hold→revu), adjective stacking before nouns, and SVO order. I will translate each exam sentence exactly using those rules.

ANSWERS:
1. si sapumude le lare si dumade le redo
2. si pasede sesuri redo le re
3. si lidade re lare si dasode le verisa si mova rale redo verisa

6. si lidede vulo sepepo si dasode rale redo verisa

8. si pasede sesuri redo re re revu si polode le
9. si daso rale redo verisa si daso rale le
10. si movade le salu si lida rale
